hooks.prompt: Assess your current context window usage based on the conversation history so far — volume of files read, edits made, tool calls, and overall message length. Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no other text. If context feels above 50% full: {"decision": "block", "reason": "Context is over 50% full. Run /compact first to compress the conversation, then I will reprocess your request."}. If context is at 50% or under: {"decision": "allow"}. Input:

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | jq -r .tool_input.file_path 2>/dev/null); [[ -z "$file" || "$file" == "null" ]] && exit 0; case "$file" in *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.jsx|*.mjs|*.cjs|*.json|*.md|*.css) command -v prettier >/dev/null 2>&1 && prettier --write "$file" --log-level silent 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac; exit 0'

hooks.prompt: The context window is approaching its limit and compaction is about to occur. Before compacting, produce a structured summary with these sections: (1) FILES: files modified, key changes, current state; (2) TESTS: what was written, pass/fail status, coverage gaps; (3) TODOs: open issues, known bugs, next steps; (4) DECISIONS: architectural or implementation choices made and the reasoning. Write this summary clearly so that a fresh context can resume this work without losing critical information. Input:

hooks.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); [[ $(echo "$input" | jq -r .stop_hook_active) == "true" ]] && exit 0; echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"Run /checkpoint to save progress before finishing.\"}"'
